surface.name: Quickstart: sandbox domain cURL send first email
surface.selection_basis: The official documentation presents this as the hosted path from account setup to Send Your First Email. It reaches the requested first-success boundary without extending into post-success production work.

entry_point.developer_intent: Mailgun accepts the sandbox-domain cURL request and delivers the first email to an authorized recipient.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has no prior Mailgun setup for this journey.
The journey starts at account creation and stops at the first documented successful outcome.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Mailgun accepts the sandbox-domain cURL request and delivers the first email to an authorized recipient.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The API returns a queued message response and the authorized recipient receives the email.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The official documentation leads the developer to: Send Your First Email.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the first observable happy-path result on the selected Quickstart: sandbox domain cURL send first email surface; later configuration and production hardening occur after success.

prerequisites.requirement: A Mailgun account with its sandbox domain.
The Mailgun API key shown for the account.
A recipient address authorized for the sandbox domain.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to Mailgun and open the sandbox-domain quick start.
primary_path.input: A new developer starting at account creation
primary_path.output: The sandbox domain and sending credentials are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The sandbox domain and sending credentials are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Authorize and verify the intended sandbox recipient if it is not already authorized.
primary_path.output: The recipient is listed as authorized.
primary_path.success_signal: The recipient is listed as authorized.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Run the documented cURL message send against the sandbox domain using API authentication.
primary_path.output: Mailgun returns a successful queued response.
primary_path.success_signal: Mailgun returns a successful queued response.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Inspect the authorized recipient inbox and confirm delivery of the first message.
primary_path.output: The sent message is visible in the inbox.
primary_path.success_signal: The sent message is visible in the inbox.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

friction_gates.description: Sandbox domains send only to authorized recipients, who may need to complete email verification.
Email delivery can be delayed or filtered by the recipient provider.

sources.title: Send Your First Email
evidence_supported: Mailgun accepts the sandbox-domain cURL request and delivers the first email to an authorized recipient.
The API returns a queued message response and the authorized recipient receives the email.

uncertainties.question: Does the current fresh-account UI exactly match every navigation label in the documented Send Your First Email path?
uncertainties.impact: Navigation wording may differ while the required outcome and boundary remain the same.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official documentation establishes the journey but account-specific UI state can vary.
